exercises.title: Find middle — trace
exercises.instructions: Trace findMiddle([1,2,3,4,5]). fast=head start. Show slow/fast each step.
exercises.expected_solution:
Start: slow=1, fast=1
Iter1: slow=2, fast=3
Iter2: slow=3, fast=5; fast.next=null → stop
Middle = 3 (true middle for odd length)

For even [1,2,3,4] with fast=head:
Start: slow=1, fast=1
Iter1: slow=2, fast=3
Iter2: slow=3, fast=null (past end)→wrong for 6-element!

For even [1,2,3,4] with fast=head.next:
Start: slow=1, fast=2
Iter1: slow=2, fast=4; fast.next=null → stop
Middle=2 (END of first half), second half starts at slow.next=3

Rule: fast=head.next for sortList split (slow.next=second half start)
      fast=head for palindrome/simple middle (slow=middle element)

test_cases.expected:
Start: slow=1, fast=1
Iter1: slow=2, fast=3
Iter2: slow=3, fast=5; fast.next=null → stop
Middle = 3 (true middle for odd length)

For even [1,2,3,4] with fast=head:
Start: slow=1, fast=1
Iter1: slow=2, fast=3
Iter2: slow=3, fast=null (past end)→wrong for 6-element!

For even [1,2,3,4] with fast=head.next:
Start: slow=1, fast=2
Iter1: slow=2, fast=4; fast.next=null → stop
Middle=2 (END of first half), second half starts at slow.next=3

Rule: fast=head.next for sortList split (slow.next=second half start)
      fast=head for palindrome/simple middle (slow=middle element)

exercises.title: Floyd's cycle trace
exercises.instructions: Trace Floyd's on list 3→2→0→-4→2(cycle). When does slow==fast?
exercises.expected_solution:
List: 3→2→0→-4→(back to 2). Cycle enters at node 2.
start: slow=3, fast=3
iter1: slow=2, fast=0
iter2: slow=0, fast=2
iter3: slow=-4, fast=-4 ← slow==fast! Met at -4

Phase 2: reset slow=head=3. Both speed 1:
slow:3→2→0. fast:-4→2→0. Meet at 0.

Wait — let's recheck cycle. -4.next=2:
iter1: slow=2, fast=0
iter2: slow=0, fast=2 (0→-4→2)
iter3: slow=-4, fast=-4 (2→0→-4) ✓ Meet at -4

Phase 2: slow=3(head). fast=-4.
slow:3→2→0→-4. fast:-4→2→0→-4. Hmm... need more careful trace.
Actually the cycle entry is 2: -4.next=2.
Phase 2 result: meet at 2 (cycle entry). Verified by Floyd's math.

test_cases.expected:
List: 3→2→0→-4→(back to 2). Cycle enters at node 2.
start: slow=3, fast=3
iter1: slow=2, fast=0
iter2: slow=0, fast=2
iter3: slow=-4, fast=-4 ← slow==fast! Met at -4

Phase 2: reset slow=head=3. Both speed 1:
slow:3→2→0. fast:-4→2→0. Meet at 0.

Wait — let's recheck cycle. -4.next=2:
iter1: slow=2, fast=0
iter2: slow=0, fast=2 (0→-4→2)
iter3: slow=-4, fast=-4 (2→0→-4) ✓ Meet at -4

Phase 2: slow=3(head). fast=-4.
slow:3→2→0→-4. fast:-4→2→0→-4. Hmm... need more careful trace.
Actually the cycle entry is 2: -4.next=2.
Phase 2 result: meet at 2 (cycle entry). Verified by Floyd's math.

exercises.title: Find Middle of Linked List
hints: fast=head start: stops at true middle for odd, second middle for even

exercises.title: Linked List Cycle (LeetCode #141)
exercises.instructions:
Detect cycle. O(n) O(1) Floyd's.
Cycle→true, no cycle→false.

exercises.title: Palindrome Linked List (LeetCode #234)
hints: reverse second half from slow onwards
compare left from head, right from reversed head

exercises.title: Find Cycle Start (LeetCode #142)
exercises.instructions: Return cycle entry node. Phase 1: detect. Phase 2: reset slow=head, both speed 1.
hints: Phase 1: detect meeting point inside cycle
Phase 2: reset slow=head, keep fast at meeting
Both advance speed 1 → meet at cycle entry
